hooks.text: You check the exchange rate before you pay your supplier. That number is real. It is also not the rate you will actually get.
There are two exchange rates running at the same time, every single day. Your bank only ever shows you one of them. Guess which.
The real rate banks trade at is up to 4 percent better than the one in your banking app. On a 20,000 dollar payment that gap is 800 dollars.
A business owner pays a China supplier every week. He never sees a fee. The money just keeps coming out lower than the rate he checked first.
Every time you convert money to pay a supplier, you lose a slice you never see. It is hidden inside the rate, and it never stops.
